rulings.Case Title: REYNALDO VARGAS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of New Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus [Ccp 1094.5]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 28, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER REYNALDO VARGAS' Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus [CCP 1094.5]. Denied. (CCP § 1094.5.) First, the punitive action against Petitioner is not barred by the statute of limitations. (Gov. Code § 3304(d)(1).) The administrative record does not support a finding that Petitioner's misconduct was "discovered by a person authorized to initiate an investigation" prior to October 8, 2010. Officers Hilder and Overand were not superior officers who could initiate an investigation. (Department General Order nos. 1.06(I)(A)(4), 3.02 ("DGO").) The audit of overtime and premium pay within the police department was issued by the Office of the City Controller. (Admin. Record, p. AR000417 - AR000466.) The audit provided "28 recommendations for the department to improve how it controls the use of overtime and premium pay." (Admin Record, p. AR000419.) It did not pertain to identifying individual violators. The filing of charges on September 28, 2009 was timely and did not violate Petitioner's due process rights. Second, the decision and punishment are supported by the weight of the evidence. Petitioner was charged with 14 incidences of violating DGO no. 11.01(B)(5)(c). The punishment was not excessive. (See Deegan v. City of Mountain View (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 37, 45.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: OUR PLANET RECYCLING, SF, LLC VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF RESOURCES RECYCLING AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 28, 2014, Line 3, RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF RESOURCES RECYCLING AND RECOVERY'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. The demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff pleads an actual controversy and declaratory relief is appropriate. Even if the court agreed with defendant's interpretation of the Recycling Act, it would be improper to sustain defendant's demurrer. "In an action for declaratory relief, when the complaint shows the existence of an actual controversy among the parties, a general demurrer to the complaint should be overruled. The plaintiff is entitled to a declaration of his rights whether the declaration is favorable to him or not.'" General Ins. Co. of America v. Whitmore (1965) 235 Cal.App.2d 670, 673; see also Hawkins v. McLaughlin (1961) 196 Cal.App.2d 318, 329 ("As heretofore stated, the court should not dispose of the case on demurrer merely because the plaintiff is not entitled to a favorable declaration. * * * 'To hold that a plaintiff on the wrong side of a controversy is not entitled to the security and relief against uncertainty which a declaratory judgment affords would require us to read into the statute a limitation not there present. * * *'") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SIDNEY SILBERBERG VS. JAMESTOWN COMMUNITY CENTER INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Brian William'S Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff For Failure To Obey Court Order
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, May 28, 2014, Line 4, Defendant Brian William'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Obey Court Order Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed motion is granted. Defendant Brian Williams is dismissed. Plaintiff shall pay defendant Williams sanctions of $1,200. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highman-ball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA PETERSON VS. G. MAZZERA COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 28, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT G. MAZZERA COMPANY'S Motion For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant's motion for terminating sanctions is denied. Plaintiff's failure to pay sanctions is not the type of court order violation for which a terminating sanction may be imposed. (See Newland vs. Sup.Ct. (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 608.) That portion of the defendant's motion seeking a trial continuance is denied without prejudice; trial continuances are heard in Department 206. The Court notes Plaintiff made arrangements to complete her deposition on May 19, 2014 in San Francisco, and that date and location was agreed to by the attorney for the defendant. Both parties' request for monetary sanctions is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC., IN THE PUBLIC VS. KOHL'S CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 28, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFF CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC., IN THE PUBLIC INTEREST's Motion For Attorneys Fees. Denied. "In order to obtain attorney fees without such a judicially recognized change in the legal relationship between the parties, a plaintiff must establish that: (1) the lawsuit was a catalyst motivating the defendants to provide the primary relief sought; (2) that the lawsuit had merit and achieved its catalytic effect by threat of victory, not by dint of nuisance and threat of expense, as elaborated in Graham; and (3) that the plaintiffs reasonably attempted to settle the litigation prior to filing the lawsuit." (Tipton-Whittingham v. City of Los Angeles (2004) 34 Cal.4th 604, 608.) Plaintiff has not established that the three prongs are met here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT VS. PACFIC GAS & ELECRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 28, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to causes of action 3 and 10 as conceded. The court overrules the demurrer to cause of action 9. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads the contract according to its effect. The court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to cause of action 11 because it is not a distinct claim. The court sustains the demurrer with leave to amend as to cause of action 12. Plaintiff must plead facts showing how defendant impliedly requested the work. The demurrer to cause of action 13 is sustained with leave to amend as conceded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
